$comment: Index of the operations the bus implements. An operation is a call a client makes to the bus over the Wire API: the client invokes it and receives its result. The bus serves the call, stamps the frame around the record, and enforces identity (author) from the authenticated request — the client never sets id, author, revision, or timestamps. The backend the bus runs on is opaque: no backend operations appear here. Delivery shapes the call's result: one-shot returns once; pull-batch returns a batch plus a cursor to continue; push-stream delivers frames until stopped. Types are informal hints; 'lexicon' references protocol/lexicons/.

operations.name: message.publish
operations.summary: Publish a typed message on a subject. The bus stamps the frame (id, author, epoch) around the record.

operations.name: message.read
operations.summary: Fetch a batch of retained messages from a cursor. Cursor 0 means the beginning; next_cursor can be passed unchanged to continue.

input.subject: string (exact or wildcard)
input.since: uint64 (next sequence to read; 0 = from start)

operations.name: message.subscribe
operations.summary: Stream messages matching a subject until stopped.

operations.name: artifact.create
operations.summary: Create a new artifact from a lexicon record. Fails if the name exists. The bus stamps revision and timestamps.

operations.name: artifact.update
operations.summary: Compare-and-set update of an artifact. The bus advances the revision and stamps updatedAt.

operations.name: artifact.get
operations.summary: Read an artifact's current value and bus-stamped metadata.

operations.name: artifact.list
operations.summary: List the artifacts directory: the name and bus-stamped metadata of every artifact in the ARTIFACTS bucket, sorted by name. Discovery of existing state the bus already owns (ADR-0016) — a client lists, then artifact.gets the one it wants. Deleted artifacts are not listed; an empty bucket is an empty list, not an error.

operations.name: artifact.delete
operations.summary: Remove an artifact. Delete is unconditional in the reference surface.

operations.name: artifact.watch
operations.summary: Stream changes to an artifact: initial value, updates, and deletes.

operations.name: clients.list
operations.summary: List the clients directory: every bus-issued identity, online and offline, with its derived presence. The bus computes presence from the live connection, not a stored field (ADR-0020).

operations.name: clients.register
operations.summary: Issue a new client identity (ADR-0020). The bus mints a ULID and its credential and persists the durable record; the signing keys never leave the bus. This is the single exception to 'you must already be someone': it is authorized — not by a held client identity alone — but by the caller's authority, either a held identity (the operator, minting for another) or a bootstrap/enrollment authorization with no pre-existing identity (locality at the weak end, a signed token at the strong end; minting for self). Same mint-and-return either way; the auth mode is only the gate.

operations.name: clients.retire
operations.summary: Decommission an identity for good (ADR-0020): remove it from the directory and drop any live connection. Operator-only for now (managed-auth parents later, ADR-0009). Distinct from a disconnect, which only drops presence to offline; a clean client Close does not retire.
